verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/Hsp72) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or signal peptide. However, extensive evidence demonstrates its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically in tumor cells and heat/stress-conditioned cells, driven by binding to phosphatidylserine and the glycosphingolipid Gb3 in lipid rafts. Surface biotinylation, flow cytometry with conformation-sensitive antibodies (cmHsp70.1), and global membrane proteomics all confirm an extracellular-face-exposed pool on viable tumor cells. This is a canonical cancer ecto-chaperone surface story qualifying as cell_state_induced. Normal unstressed cells do not display HSPA1A on the outer leaflet.
